name: SOAR MCP Server
description: Exposes Splunk SOAR as an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server endpoint. Claude Desktop, Claude Code, or any MCP-compatible AI client connects directly to SOAR and uses analyst-focused tools to read cases, artifacts, playbooks, and optionally write notes or run playbooks. Configure tools directly via the asset configuration checkboxes below.

auth_token.description: SOAR authentication token (Administration > User Management > Users > Authorization Tokens). Used for Test Connectivity only.

base_url.description: Base URL of this SOAR instance (e.g. https://soar.example.com). Used for Test Connectivity only.

ai_instructions.description: Additional context sent to the AI on every MCP session (appended to the built-in instructions). Use this to describe your SOAR environment, naming conventions, severity triage rules, escalation procedures, or any SOC-specific context the AI should know. Example: 'Cases labeled phishing should always be escalated if severity is high. Owner format is firstname.lastname.'

tool_list_cases.description: READ — list_cases: List SOAR cases with status/severity/label/owner filters.

tool_get_case.description: READ — get_case: Get full details of a specific case by ID.

tool_search_cases.description: READ — search_cases: Search cases by keyword across title and description.

tool_list_artifacts.description: READ — list_artifacts: List all IOCs/observables attached to a case.

tool_get_artifact.description: READ — get_artifact: Get full details of a specific artifact by ID.

tool_list_case_notes.description: READ — list_case_notes: List analyst notes and comments on a case.

tool_list_playbooks.description: READ — list_playbooks: List available SOAR playbooks with name and description.

tool_get_playbook_run.description: READ — get_playbook_run: Get the status and results of a playbook run.

tool_list_action_runs.description: READ — list_action_runs: List recent automated action runs on a case.

tool_list_users.description: READ — list_users: List SOAR users with roles and email addresses.

tool_get_soar_info.description: READ — get_soar_info: Get SOAR version, build number, and installed app count.

tool_add_case_note.description: WRITE ⚠️ — add_case_note: Add a note/comment to a case. Modifies live SOAR data.

tool_create_artifact.description: WRITE ⚠️ — create_artifact: Add a new artifact/IOC to a case. Modifies live SOAR data.

tool_run_playbook.description: WRITE ⚠️ — run_playbook: Trigger a SOAR playbook on a case. May execute automated response actions.

tool_update_case_owner.description: WRITE ⚠️ — update_case_owner: Reassign a case to a different analyst. Modifies live SOAR data.

tool_update_case_severity.description: WRITE ⚠️ — update_case_severity: Change the severity level of a case. Modifies live SOAR data.

tool_update_case_status.description: WRITE ⚠️ — update_case_status: Change case status (open/closed/resolved/new/in_progress). Modifies live SOAR data.

scoped_tokens_enabled.description: v1.5.0+ — Enable scoped MCP tokens (per-user, revocable, optionally tool-restricted). Tokens are minted by the 'mint mcp token' action. When enabled, presented headers are first looked up in the token store; legacy SOAR ph-auth-tokens still work as a fallback unless scoped_tokens_required is also set.

scoped_tokens_required.description: v1.5.0+ — Reject any request that does not present a recognised scoped MCP token. Use after migrating all clients off shared ph-auth-tokens. Default off so upgrades do not break existing integrations.

actions.description: Verifies that the SOAR MCP Server REST handler is reachable and that the configuration is valid. Also applies asset configuration (tool checkboxes) to the running server.
actions.verbose: Checks that the MCP REST endpoint is registered, reads asset configuration (tool enable/disable, AI instructions), and writes effective config so the REST handler picks it up. Optionally verifies the SOAR auth token if base_url and auth_token are set.

actions.description: Returns the current MCP server configuration including which tools are enabled and the AI instructions. The result widget auto-generates ready-to-copy Claude Desktop and Claude Code JSON config snippets.
actions.verbose: Reads the effective configuration (asset config checkboxes + mcp.conf overrides) and returns the resolved state. The custom widget renders a Config Builder tab with copy buttons for Claude Desktop and Claude Code JSON snippets.

actions.description: Mint a scoped MCP token bound to a specific SOAR user. The raw token is returned ONCE in the action result and must be copied immediately. Subsequent requests from the MCP client that present this token will be authorised as the bound user with the optional tool allow-list.
actions.verbose: Creates a new entry in the app's local token store. The bound SOAR ph-auth-token is encrypted at rest with a Fernet key generated on first mint. allowed_tools should be a comma-separated list of tool names or left blank to permit all asset-enabled tools.

soar_user_id.description: SOAR user identifier the token acts on behalf of.

bound_soar_auth_token.description: The SOAR ph-auth-token used downstream when calling SOAR APIs. If omitted, falls back to the asset config auth_token.

allowed_tools.description: Comma-separated tool allow-list (e.g. 'list_cases,get_case,list_artifacts'). Blank = all asset-enabled tools.

lifetime_days.description: Token validity in days. 0 means never expires.

actions.description: List all scoped MCP tokens in the store with metadata (id, label, allowed tools, last-used, expiry). Never returns raw tokens or their hashes.

include_revoked.description: If true, include revoked tokens in the listing.

actions.description: Revoke a scoped MCP token by ID. The token is immediately invalidated; subsequent requests presenting it will be rejected with a JSON-RPC -32000 'revoked' error.

token_id.description: Token ID to revoke (from 'list mcp tokens').
